title: Schefflera systyla

It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The genus Schefflera is named after Johann Scheffler, a German poet and theologian.
